facts.text: West Hempstead got its name when the Long Island Rail Road built a station west of Hempstead in 1891, turning “West of Hempstead” into the town’s title.
West Hempstead Public Library opened in 1956 and received state charter in 1967, becoming a cornerstone of local learning.
Hempstead Avenue hosts a strip of mom‑and‑pop shops, kosher eateries, and Riesterer’s Bakery, which opened its doors in 1931.
Halls Pond Park, the main green space in West Hempstead, was dedicated by Nassau County in 1961 and still serves residents today.
In 1906, artists William Balfour Ker and Mary Ellen Sigsbee bought a country home just south of the current Lakeview library, a landmark of early 20th‑century art.
